title: [Epic] CQRS — Separate Commands from Queries & Add Read Models
body:
## Issue description

The API currently mixes command (mutating) and query (read-only) endpoints in a single route module. All reads go through `PlayerRepository.findById()`, which performs a heavy 6-table JOIN to reconstruct the full aggregate — even when the client only needs a summary or a single field. There are no read-optimized projections, making it impossible to serve different clients (game UI, admin dashboard, leaderboard) efficiently.

## High-level requirements

This epic tracks the work to:
1. **Separate command routes from query routes** at the API layer, establishing a clear CQRS boundary.
2. **Introduce read-model projections** — denormalized, query-optimized views of player state maintained asynchronously.
3. **Add a leaderboard / social read model** as the first cross-player projection.

## Additional notes

- Current routes are all in `apps/api/src/routes/index.ts`. Commands (POST) and queries (GET) are interleaved.
- The application layer currently has no query handlers — only command handlers in `packages/application/src/commands/`.
- Read models can be populated by writing to a denormalized table in the same DB transaction (simple projection) or by consuming outbox events (async projection). Start with the simple approach.
- No separate read database is needed initially — denormalized tables in the same PostgreSQL instance are sufficient.
- Sequenced: Split routes (Immediate) → Player read model (Next) → Leaderboard (Later).

### Child issues
- Split API routes into command and query modules (Immediate)
- Introduce read-model projection for player state (Next)
- Add leaderboard / social read model (Later)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Command and query routes are in separate modules with distinct URL prefixes or file structure
- [ ] At least one read-model projection exists with its own optimized schema
- [ ] Read endpoints serve from projections, not from the aggregate reconstruction path
- [ ] A leaderboard read model demonstrates cross-player querying
- [ ] Architecture docs describe the CQRS boundary and projection update strategy
